interface.data_shapes: Same Cell / Player / Board / GameState / Outcome shapes as defined in rules-engine--ai-opponent.json. The controller imports the same module; shapes are not duplicated.

methods_used.name: createBoard
methods_used.usage: Called on initial page load and on Restart to produce a fresh GameState.

methods_used.name: applyMove
methods_used.usage: Called after each human click and each AI move. Controller passes (state, index, currentPlayer) and receives a new state to render.

methods_used.name: checkOutcome
methods_used.usage: Called after every applyMove to determine whether to continue, declare a winner, or declare a draw.

methods_used.name: legalMoves
methods_used.usage: Optional — controller may consult to short-circuit illegal-click handling, but is not required to (applyMove's error contract is sufficient).

guarantees: Module is loadable in a vanilla browser context with no build step.
Module exposes a stable named export surface (window-global or ES-module export — Builder must document which and the controller adopts the same loading convention).

notes: Controller treats rules-engine as the single source of truth. It must not maintain its own duplicate board representation; it should hold the GameState returned by rules-engine and pass it to ui-render via a view-model adapter.
